title: wdmatch
subject:
Write a program that takes two strings and prints the second one, followed by a newline, only if the first string can be written using the characters of the second string in order (the first string is a subsequence of the second). Otherwise print just a newline.

If the number of arguments is not exactly 2, print just a newline.

Examples:
  $ ./a.out "abc" "aXbXc"
  aXbXc
  $ ./a.out "az" "ba"
  (empty line)
